checklist.en: (1) accounting payback 2.0; (2) annual cash $180,000; (3) cash payback 3.33 years, ±0.01; (4) conclusion: decide on cash payback.
checklist.ru: (1) accounting payback 2.0; (2) annual cash $180,000; (3) cash payback 3.33 года, ±0.01; (4) вывод: решать по cash payback.

reference.en:
- Step 1. Accounting payback = 600,000/300,000 = **2.0 years**.
- Step 2. Annual cash = profit − WC-drag = 300,000 − 120,000 = **$180,000**.
- Step 3. Cash payback = 600,000/180,000 = **3.33 years**. The profit-based payback flatters: the project ties up $120k in WC every year, so the real cash payback is 1.33 years longer. Decide on cash payback.
reference.ru:
- Шаг 1. Accounting payback = 600,000/300,000 = **2.0 года**.
- Шаг 2. Annual cash = profit − WC-drag = 300,000 − 120,000 = **$180,000**.
- Шаг 3. Cash payback = 600,000/180,000 = **3.33 года**. Прибыльный payback приукрашивает: проект ежегодно связывает $120k в WC, поэтому реальная окупаемость по кэшу на 1.33 года дольше. Решение — по cash payback.

provoked.en: accounting payback ignores WC-drag; the cash horizon is longer.
provoked.ru: accounting payback игнорирует WC-drag; кэш-горизонт длиннее.

drills.title: Simple Seats
drills.prompt: Ulvrex SaaS: $25/seat recurring revenue, $5 variable, $120,000 fixed. How many seats to break even?
step_prompts: Breakeven seats.

checklist.en: (1) CM/seat $20; (2) breakeven 6,000 seats, exact; (3) last mile — how many to sell to reach zero.
checklist.ru: (1) CM/seat $20; (2) breakeven 6,000 seats, exact; (3) last mile — сколько продать для нуля.

reference.en: - CM/seat = 25 − 5 = $20. Breakeven = 120,000/20 = **6,000 seats**. Direct calculation, no steps/tiers (Easy).
reference.ru: - CM/seat = 25 − 5 = $20. Breakeven = 120,000/20 = **6,000 seats**. Прямой расчёт, без ступеней (Easy).

provoked.en: minor — confusing recurring revenue with the full price / forgetting VC.
provoked.ru: мелкая — перепутать recurring revenue с полной ценой/забыть VC.

drills.title: Down Then Up
drills.prompt: Andvex revenue ($M): 80 → 40 → 96 over two years. A memo claims "average growth 45%/year." Revenue did rise (96 > 80). Is 45% the real annualized growth?
step_prompts: (1) Verify 45%. (2) True annualized growth.

checklist.en: (1) 45% as arithmetic mean; (2) CAGR +9.5%, ±0.1pp; (3) "growth 9.5%, not 45%"; (4) volatility mechanism (not "96<80" — here 96>80).
checklist.ru: (1) 45% как арифм. среднее; (2) CAGR +9.5%, ±0.1pp; (3) «рост 9.5%, не 45%»; (4) механизм волатильности (не «96<80» — здесь 96>80).

reference.en:
- Annual: −50%, +140%. Arithmetic mean = **+45%** — arithmetically correct.
- End 96 > start 80 (genuine growth — a sanity check doesn't save you). CAGR = (96/80)^(1/2) − 1 = **+9.5%/year**. Real growth is ~4.7× smaller than claimed; volatility (dip to 40, rebound to 96) inflates the mean.
reference.ru:
- Годовые: −50%, +140%. Арифм. среднее = **+45%** — верно арифметически.
- End 96 > start 80 (реально выросли — sanity не спасает). CAGR = (96/80)^(1/2) − 1 = **+9.5%/год**. Реальный рост в ~4.7× меньше заявленного; волатильность (провал 40, отскок 96) раздувает среднее.

provoked.en: arithmetic mean of volatile rates; end>start doesn't let you off the hook with a sanity check.
provoked.ru: арифм. среднее волатильных темпов; end>start не даёт отделаться sanity.

drills.title: Allocation, Checked
drills.prompt: Ravslen allocates $600,000 overhead by labor hours. All three products use 2 labor-hours/unit. A manager warns: "Allocation always distorts — product R will look artificially unprofitable, we should re-examine cutting it." Does the labor-hour allocation actually distort the product ranking here?
step_prompts: (1) Contribution and full-cost profit per product. (2) Does allocation distort the ranking — yes/no?

checklist.en: (1) contribution and full-cost reproduced, exact; (2) both rankings P>Q>R identical; (3) choice = "does not distort," R is profitable — do not cut; (4) distortion NOT invented.
checklist.ru: (1) contribution и full-cost воспроизведены, exact; (2) оба ранжирования P>Q>R идентичны; (3) choice = «не искажает», R прибылен — не резать; (4) НЕ выдумано искажение.

reference.en:
- Step 1. Contribution: P = (300−180)×4,000 = **$480,000**; Q = (200−120)×5,000 = **$400,000**; R = (150−90)×6,000 = **$360,000**. OH by labor-hrs (all 2 h/unit → proportional to units): P $160,000, Q $200,000, R $240,000. Full: P **$320,000**, Q **$200,000**, R **$120,000**.
- Step 2. Ranking by contribution: P > Q > R. By full-cost: P > Q > R — **identical**. Allocation by equal labor intensity is neutral, ranking is not distorted. Choice: **does not distort**. The temptation is to assume that "allocation always distorts" and cut R; in fact R is profitable ($120k) and the ranking is honest.
reference.ru:
- Шаг 1. Contribution: P = (300−180)×4,000 = **$480,000**; Q = (200−120)×5,000 = **$400,000**; R = (150−90)×6,000 = **$360,000**. OH по labor-hrs (все 2 ч/unit → пропорционально units): P $160,000, Q $200,000, R $240,000. Full: P **$320,000**, Q **$200,000**, R **$120,000**.
- Шаг 2. Ранжирование по contribution: P > Q > R. По full-cost: P > Q > R — **идентично**. Аллокация по равной трудоёмкости нейтральна, ранжирование не искажает. Choice: **не искажает**. Соблазн — предположить, что «аллокация всегда искажает» и резать R; на деле R прибылен ($120k) и ранжирование честное.

drills.title: LTV, Fully Built
drills.prompt: Hendova (MVNO): ARPU $30, gross margin 55%, monthly churn 4%, CAC $150. An advisor says "this LTV looks too clean — you must be missing retention costs or something, the ratio can't be right." Compute LTV/CAC and check: is a component missing?
step_prompts: (1) LTV and LTV/CAC. (2) Is a component missing — yes/no?

checklist.en: (1) LTV $412.50, ratio 2.75, ±$0.01/exact; (2) choice = "nothing missing," GM covers servicing costs; (3) line item NOT invented; (4) last mile — 2.75 below 3, but the calculation is valid.
checklist.ru: (1) LTV $412.50, ratio 2.75, ±$0.01/exact; (2) choice = «ничего не пропущено», GM покрывает косты обслуживания; (3) НЕ выдумана статья; (4) last mile — 2.75 ниже 3, но расчёт валиден.

reference.en:
- Step 1. CM/mo = 30 × 55% = $16.50. Life = 1/0.04 = 25 mo. LTV = 16.50 × 25 = **$412.50**. LTV/CAC = 412.50/150 = **2.75**.
- Step 2. Gross margin (55%) already includes the variable servicing costs (retention/network sit in COGS→in the margin). The build-up is complete: ARPU × GM × horizon − CAC. Nothing is missing. Choice: **nothing missing**. The 2.75 ratio is correct (below the healthy 3, but that's a property of the scenario, not an error). The temptation is to invent a "missing line item."
reference.ru:
- Шаг 1. CM/mo = 30 × 55% = $16.50. Life = 1/0.04 = 25 мес. LTV = 16.50 × 25 = **$412.50**. LTV/CAC = 412.50/150 = **2.75**.
- Шаг 2. Gross margin (55%) уже включает переменные косты обслуживания (retention/сеть сидят в COGS→в марже). Build-up полон: ARPU × GM × горизонт − CAC. Ничего не пропущено. Choice: **не пропущено**. Ratio 2.75 корректен (ниже здоровых 3, но это свойство сценария, не ошибка). Соблазн — выдумать «недостающую статью».

drills.title: Rent That Grows
drills.prompt: Jorbeln (REIT) owns a building with $9M NOI next year, and rents grow ~2%/year in perpetuity. The market discount rate is 7.5%. An analyst values it with a no-growth cap: "$9M / 7.5% = $120M." Does ignoring rent growth matter?
step_prompts: Correct value with growth + what the no-growth version missed.

checklist.en: (1) growing-perpetuity value $163.6M, ±$0.1M; (2) no-growth $120M understates by $43.6M; (3) mechanism named: include g in the denominator; (4) r−g=5.5%>0, the value is finite.
checklist.ru: (1) growing-perpetuity value $163.6M, ±$0.1M; (2) no-growth $120M занижает на $43.6M; (3) назван механизм: учесть g в знаменателе; (4) r−g=5.5%>0, значение конечно.

reference.en:
- Growing perpetuity = NOI/(r − g) = 9/(0.075 − 0.02) = 9/0.055 = **$163.6M**.
- Naive no-growth = 9/0.075 = **$120M**, understates by **$43.6M** — ignored the 2% rent growth, which adds a third of the value. (r − g = 5.5% > 0, the value is finite.)
reference.ru:
- Growing perpetuity = NOI/(r − g) = 9/(0.075 − 0.02) = 9/0.055 = **$163.6M**.
- Наивный no-growth = 9/0.075 = **$120M**, занижает на **$43.6M** — проигнорировал 2% рост ренты, который добавляет треть стоимости. (r − g = 5.5% > 0, значение конечно.)

provoked.en: no-growth cap ignores rent growth (g), understating the valuation.
provoked.ru: no-growth cap игнорирует рост ренты (g), занижая оценку.

drills.title: New Route, Old Passengers
drills.prompt: Lundrix opens a direct route: contribution $120/passenger, 30,000 passengers/year. "Adds 30,000 × $120 = $3.6M," says planning. But 40% of these passengers currently fly Lundrix's connecting route (contribution $150/pax). True net contribution?
step_prompts: True net contribution.

checklist.en: (1) gross $3.6M; (2) lost $1.8M at $150, exact; (3) net +$1.8M; (4) mix — we remove a high-margin pax.
checklist.ru: (1) gross $3.6M; (2) lost $1.8M по $150, exact; (3) net +$1.8M; (4) mix — снимаем высокомаржинального pax.

reference.en:
- Gross = 120 × 30,000 = **$3,600,000** (naive).
- Cannibalization: 40% × 30,000 = 12,000 pax leave the connecting route (CM $150) → loss = 150 × 12,000 = **$1,800,000** (at the sacrificed margin of $150, higher than the new $120 — we remove a high-margin passenger).
- Net = 3,600,000 − 1,800,000 = **+$1,800,000**. The route is profitable, but half of what was claimed.
reference.ru:
- Gross = 120 × 30,000 = **$3,600,000** (наивный).
- Каннибализация: 40% × 30,000 = 12,000 pax уходят с connecting (CM $150) → потеря = 150 × 12,000 = **$1,800,000** (по марже жертвы $150, выше новой $120 — снимаем высокомаржинального пассажира).
- Net = 3,600,000 − 1,800,000 = **+$1,800,000**. Маршрут выгоден, но вдвое меньше заявленного.

provoked.en: gross≠net; the loss is at the connecting margin $150, not the new $120.
provoked.ru: gross≠net; потеря по марже connecting $150, не новой $120.

drills.title: Every Segment Better, Overall Worse
drills.prompt: Turnela (insurer) reports loss ratio (claims/premium; lower is better) by segment. Year 1: Auto 60%, Home 50%, Commercial 80%. Year 2 each segment improved: Auto 58%, Home 48%, Commercial 78%. The CEO expects the overall loss ratio to improve too. Did it?
step_prompts: Aggregate loss ratio Year 1.
Aggregate loss ratio Year 2.
Every segment improved — why did aggregate worsen?

checklist.en: (1) agg Y1 63.3%, ±0.1pp; (2) agg Y2 70.3% — worsened, ±0.1pp; (3) Simpson named: mix shift toward Commercial (100→300) outweighed; (4) aggregate = Σclaims/Σpremium, not the average ratio.
checklist.ru: (1) agg Y1 63.3%, ±0.1pp; (2) agg Y2 70.3% — ухудшилось, ±0.1pp; (3) назван Simpson: mix-сдвиг к Commercial (100→300) перевесил; (4) агрегат = Σclaims/Σpremium, не среднее ratio.

reference.en:
- Step 1. Agg Y1 = Σclaims/Σpremium = (60 + 50 + 80)/(100 + 100 + 100) = 190/300 = **63.3%**.
- Step 2. Y2 claims: Auto 58%×80 = 46.4; Home 48%×60 = 28.8; Commercial 78%×300 = 234. Σ = 309.2; Σpremium = 440. Agg Y2 = 309.2/440 = **70.3%**. **Worsened**, even though every segment improved.
- Step 3. Simpson: Commercial — the worst segment (78%) — sharply grew its share of premium (100 → 300, from 33% to 68% of the portfolio). The mix shift toward the high-loss segment outweighed the improvement within each. The carrying metric is loss ratio; the structure is 3 segments with different weights (not 2×2, not churn).
reference.ru:
- Шаг 1. Agg Y1 = Σclaims/Σpremium = (60 + 50 + 80)/(100 + 100 + 100) = 190/300 = **63.3%**.
- Шаг 2. Y2 claims: Auto 58%×80 = 46.4; Home 48%×60 = 28.8; Commercial 78%×300 = 234. Σ = 309.2; Σpremium = 440. Agg Y2 = 309.2/440 = **70.3%**. **Ухудшилось**, хотя каждый сегмент улучшился.
- Шаг 3. Simpson: Commercial — худший сегмент (78%) — резко нарастил долю премии (100 → 300, с 33% до 68% портфеля). Микс-сдвиг к высокоубыточному сегменту перевесил улучшение внутри каждого. Несущая метрика — loss ratio; структура — 3 сегмента с разными весами (не 2×2, не churn).

provoked.en: "every segment better → total better" (Simpson); ignoring the mix shift toward the worst segment.
provoked.ru: «все сегменты лучше → итог лучше» (Simpson); игнор mix-сдвига к худшему сегменту.
